notes: MCP-layer DQ rules for get_ai_exposure tool. 5 rules covering eval pass rate, tool registration, null handling, response completeness, and response time. Gold-zone data quality is enforced by governance/dq-rules/gold-ai-exposure.json (15 rules); these MCP rules cover the serving layer only.

rules.name: Eval set pass rate >= 80%
rules.test: Run all 62 eval cases from data/ai_ready/eval/mcp-ai-exposure-eval.json against the MCP tool. Compare actual responses to expected_answer for each case.
rules.threshold: pass_rate >= 0.80 (at least 50 of 62 cases must pass)
rules.description: The MCP tool must correctly answer at least 80% of the mechanically verifiable eval set. Covers point lookups, comparisons, rankings, aggregations, and edge cases.
rules.rationale: Eval set has 62 cases across 5 categories (34 point_lookup, 8 comparison, 5 ranking, 7 aggregation, 8 edge_case). 80% threshold per Brightsmith MCP convention allows tolerance for aggregation/ranking cases that depend on LLM interpretation while requiring all point lookups and edge cases to pass.

rules.name: Tool registered and callable
rules.test: Invoke get_ai_exposure with a known-valid SOC code (e.g., '13-2011'). Verify the response contains a non-null 'data' object.
rules.description: The MCP tool must be registered with the server, accept requests, and return a valid response for known SOC codes. A null data response for a valid SOC code indicates the tool is broken or the table is inaccessible.
rules.rationale: Basic liveness check. If the tool cannot serve a known-valid SOC code, no downstream consumer (Gemma agent, frontend) can function.

rules.name: Null case handled (soc_code not found)
rules.test: Invoke get_ai_exposure with an invalid SOC code (e.g., '99-9999'). Verify response.data is null and response.message is a non-empty string.
rules.threshold: response.data is null AND response.message is non-empty string
rules.description: When a SOC code is not found in consumable.ai_exposure, the tool must return a structured null response with a descriptive message rather than throwing an error or returning malformed JSON.
rules.rationale: Eval set includes 5 null-case edge cases (invalid format, nonexistent SOC, empty string, partial code, no-hyphen format). The tool must degrade gracefully for all of these.

rules.name: Response contains all 7 required fields
rules.test: Invoke get_ai_exposure with a known-valid SOC code. Verify response.data contains exactly the 7 fields: soc_code, occupation_title, exposure_score, stat_res, boss_ai_score, rationale, category.
rules.threshold: all 7 fields present and non-null in response.data
rules.description: Every successful response must contain all 7 response fields. Missing fields would break downstream consumers that expect the full schema.
rules.rationale: The 7 fields are defined in AI_EXPOSURE_RESPONSE_FIELDS in futureproof_server.py. Gold table guarantees all are NOT NULL. Any missing field at MCP layer indicates a projection or serialization bug.

rules.name: Response time reasonable
rules.test: Invoke get_ai_exposure with a known-valid SOC code. Measure wall-clock response time.
rules.description: Point lookup against 389 rows should complete within 5 seconds. This is a generous threshold to account for cold starts and network variability. Typical response should be under 500ms.
rules.rationale: The tool queries a 389-row table with a single equality filter. Cold start of Iceberg table scan is the main latency factor. 5s threshold is a degraded-performance ceiling, not a target.
